metadata.name: sss_token
metadata.description: Solana Stablecoin Standard — SSS-1 (Minimal) and SSS-2 (Compliant) presets

docs: Solana Stablecoin Standard — SSS-1 (Minimal) + SSS-2 (Compliant) + SSS-3 (Reserve-Backed)
SSS-1: Token-2022 mint with freeze authority + metadata
SSS-2: SSS-1 + permanent delegate + transfer hook + blacklist enforcement
SSS-3: SSS-1 + collateral reserve vault (deposit/redeem against on-chain reserves)

instructions.name: accept_authority
docs: Accept a pending authority transfer (two-step). Caller must be pending_authority.

accounts.name: pending
docs: Must be the pending_authority

instructions.name: accept_compliance_authority
docs: Accept a pending compliance authority transfer. Caller must be pending_compliance_authority.

accounts.name: pending
docs: Must be the pending_compliance_authority

instructions.name: add_authorized_keeper
docs: BUG-015: Add a keeper pubkey to the stability-fee keeper whitelist.

instructions.name: add_yield_collateral_mint
docs: Add a yield-bearing SPL token mint to the whitelist.
`FLAG_YIELD_COLLATERAL` must already be enabled.  Authority only.
Rejects duplicates and enforces the 8-mint cap.

instructions.name: attest_legal_entity
docs: Attestor-only: co-sign the IssuerRegistry, marking it as attested.

accounts.name: attestor
docs: Must match registry.attestor

instructions.name: blacklist_add_and_freeze
docs: BUG-022: Add wallet to blacklist AND atomically freeze its token account.
This is the primary blacklist operation for SSS-2 mints. Calling this instead
of the transfer-hook's `blacklist_add` directly ensures there is no window
between the blacklist write and the freeze — the wallet cannot front-run the
blacklist by moving tokens to a clean wallet.
Steps performed atomically in one transaction:
1. CPI → transfer-hook `blacklist_add(wallet)` to record in BlacklistState.
2. Token-2022 `freeze_account` on the wallet's token account (config PDA signer).
For pre-emptive blacklisting (wallet has no token account yet), call
`blacklist_add` on the transfer-hook program directly.

accounts.name: compliance_authority
docs: Compliance authority — must match config.compliance_authority.

accounts.name: config
docs: StablecoinConfig PDA — freeze authority and compliance authority validator.
The config PDA is the freeze authority on the Token-2022 mint.

accounts.name: mint
docs: The Token-2022 mint — freeze authority is config PDA.

accounts.name: target_token_account
docs: Token account owned by the wallet being blacklisted.
This account is frozen atomically with the blacklist write.

accounts.name: blacklist_state
docs: BlacklistState PDA on the transfer-hook program.
Seeds: [b"blacklist-state", mint] — owned by the transfer-hook program.
We pass this as mutable so the CPI to transfer-hook can write the new entry.

accounts.name: transfer_hook_program
docs: The transfer-hook program — invoked via CPI to add to blacklist.

instructions.name: burn
docs: Burn tokens from source. Caller must be a registered minter.

instructions.name: burn_accrued_fees
docs: SSS-092: Burn previously accrued stability fees from debtor's token account.
BUG-012 CRIT-07: resets `accrued_fees` to 0 after burn to prevent double-count.
Requires debtor signature.

accounts.name: debtor
docs: The CDP owner (debtor) — must sign.

instructions.name: cancel_redemption
docs: Cancel a pending redemption entry. Only the original redeemer may cancel.
Returns locked stable tokens to the redeemer.

accounts.name: escrow_stable

accounts.name: user_stable_ata

instructions.name: cancel_timelocked_op

instructions.name: cdp_borrow_stable

Enforces min 150% collateral ratio via Pyth oracle price.

Each (user, collateral_mint) pair gets its own CollateralVault PDA.

instructions.name: cdp_liquidate
docs: CDP: Liquidate an undercollateralised position (ratio < 120%).
Callable by anyone; liquidator burns full debt and receives all collateral.
Liquidate an undercollateralised CDP position.
SSS-100: Extended with partial liquidation and per-collateral config support.
`params.min_collateral_amount`: slippage protection (0 = disabled, backward compatible).
`params.partial_repay_amount`: if > 0, only burn this much debt and seize proportional
collateral+bonus, restoring the CDP to healthy ratio.  0 = full liquidation.

instructions.name: clear_feature_flag
docs: Clear a feature flag bit. Authority only. Pass the FLAG_* constant value.

instructions.name: clear_spend_limit
docs: Clear the spend limit and disable FLAG_SPEND_POLICY. Authority only.

instructions.name: close_verification_record
docs: Close an expired `VerificationRecord` PDA, returning rent to authority.
Fails if the record has not yet expired.  Authority only.
Users cannot be forcibly de-verified before their record expires.

accounts.name: record_owner
docs: The user whose record is being closed (rent returned to authority)

instructions.name: collect_stability_fee
docs: SSS-092: Accrue stability fees on a CDP position (keeper-callable, no burn).
BUG-012 HIGH-05: debtor does NOT need to sign — any keeper can force accrual.
Fees are accrued to `accrued_fees`; actual burn occurs on repay or via
`burn_accrued_fees` (requires debtor signature).

accounts.name: caller
docs: BUG-015: caller can be authority OR a whitelisted keeper — NOT the debtor.
The debtor no longer needs to sign; keepers may call this permissionlessly
as long as they appear in `config.authorized_keepers` (or are the authority).

accounts.name: debtor
docs: CDP position to accrue fees on — keyed by [SEED, mint, debtor_pubkey].
The `debtor` CHECK account (read-only, no signature required) is used
only to derive the PDA seeds.

accounts.name: debtor_sss_account
docs: Debtor's SSS token account — fees are burned from here.

instructions.name: cpi_burn
docs: Standardized CPI burn entrypoint.
External programs should call this instead of `burn` for forward-compatible integration.

accounts.name: interface_version
docs: InterfaceVersion PDA — validated before execution.

instructions.name: cpi_mint
docs: Standardized CPI mint entrypoint.
External programs should call this instead of `mint` for forward-compatible integration.
`required_version` must match the on-chain InterfaceVersion — guards against silent breaks.

accounts.name: minter
docs: The minter (signer) — must be a registered minter for this stablecoin.

accounts.name: interface_version
docs: InterfaceVersion PDA — caller-supplied; validated against expected seeds.

instructions.name: deposit_collateral
docs: Deposit collateral into the reserve vault (SSS-3 only).

accounts.name: sss_mint
docs: The SSS stablecoin mint (identifies which config to update)

accounts.name: collateral_mint
docs: The collateral token mint (e.g. USDC)

accounts.name: depositor_collateral
docs: Depositor's collateral token account (source)

accounts.name: reserve_vault
docs: The reserve vault — holds collateral on behalf of the stablecoin config PDA

instructions.name: draw_insurance
docs: Governance-controlled: draw from the insurance vault to cover protocol losses.
Requires authority (+ DAO quorum when FLAG_DAO_COMMITTEE is set).

accounts.name: vault_token_account
docs: Vault token account (source).

accounts.name: destination_token_account
docs: Destination token account (reserve vault or recovery account).

accounts.name: vault_authority
docs: Vault PDA — signs the transfer out of vault_token_account.

instructions.name: enqueue_redemption
docs: Enqueue a redemption request. Caller locks `amount` stable tokens into
a per-entry escrow and records the slot for front-run protection.

accounts.name: user_stable_ata
docs: User's stable token account.

accounts.name: escrow_stable
docs: Escrow: per-entry escrow account.
Seeds: [b"queue-escrow", sss_mint, queue_index.to_le_bytes()]
Authority = redemption_queue PDA.

accounts.name: slot_hashes
docs: SlotHashes sysvar — used to capture slot_hash_seed for front-run protection.

instructions.name: execute_action
docs: Execute a passed governance proposal.
Verifies that `votes.len() >= quorum` and then applies the action
(pause, feature flag change, etc.) to the StablecoinConfig.
Can be called by anyone once quorum is reached; one-shot (idempotent after execution).

accounts.name: executor
docs: Anyone can call execute once quorum is reached.

instructions.name: execute_timelocked_op
docs: Execute a pending timelocked admin operation after the delay has elapsed.

instructions.name: freeze_account
docs: Freeze an account (compliance action). Caller must be compliance authority.

instructions.name: guardian_lift_pause
docs: Lift a guardian-imposed pause.
BUG-018: Authority alone cannot lift a guardian-initiated pause until
GUARDIAN_PAUSE_AUTHORITY_OVERRIDE_DELAY (24h) has elapsed.
Full guardian quorum can always lift immediately.

accounts.name: caller
docs: Either the stablecoin authority OR a guardian (for full-quorum unpause)

instructions.name: guardian_propose_pause
docs: Any registered guardian proposes an emergency pause.
Creates a PauseProposal PDA.  If threshold == 1 the pause is immediate.

instructions.name: guardian_vote_pause
docs: Cast a YES vote on an open pause proposal.
When votes >= threshold the mint is paused immediately.
BUG-018: sets guardian_pause_active=true and starts the authority-override timelock.

instructions.name: init_dao_committee
docs: Initialize the DAO committee for a stablecoin config.
Registers `members` (1–10 pubkeys) as committee voters and sets the
`quorum` threshold.  Atomically enables FLAG_DAO_COMMITTEE.
Authority only; can only be called once per config (PDA is `init`).

instructions.name: init_guardian_config
docs: Initialise the guardian multisig for a stablecoin.
Registers 1–7 guardian pubkeys and a vote threshold.
Authority only; can only be called once.

instructions.name: init_insurance_vault
docs: Authority-only: initialise the InsuranceVault PDA and set
FLAG_INSURANCE_VAULT_REQUIRED.  Minting is blocked until the vault is
adequately seeded.

accounts.name: vault_token_account
docs: Collateral token account that will hold insurance reserves.
Must use the collateral mint matching this config.

instructions.name: init_interface_version
docs: Initialize the InterfaceVersion PDA for this mint.
One-time call by the stablecoin authority; required before `cpi_mint`/`cpi_burn`.

instructions.name: init_redemption_queue
docs: Initialise the RedemptionQueue PDA for a stablecoin.
Requires FLAG_REDEMPTION_QUEUE to be set on the StablecoinConfig.

instructions.name: init_yield_collateral
docs: Initialize yield-bearing collateral support for a stablecoin config.
Creates the `YieldCollateralConfig` PDA and atomically enables
`FLAG_YIELD_COLLATERAL`.  Only valid for SSS-3 presets.  Authority only.
`initial_mints`: optional list of yield-bearing SPL token mints to
whitelist immediately (e.g. stSOL, mSOL).  Max 8 total.

instructions.name: init_zk_compliance
docs: Initialize ZK compliance support for a stablecoin config.
Creates the `ZkComplianceConfig` PDA and atomically enables
`FLAG_ZK_COMPLIANCE`.  Only valid for SSS-2 presets (requires transfer hook).
Authority only.
`ttl_slots`: proof validity window in slots (0 = use default 1500 slots,
~10 minutes at 400ms/slot).

instructions.name: initialize
docs: Initialize a new stablecoin.
preset = 1 => SSS-1 (minimal)
preset = 2 => SSS-2 (compliant, requires transfer_hook_program)

accounts.name: mint
docs: The new Token-2022 mint — created manually so we can initialize
DefaultAccountState=Frozen *before* InitializeMint (SSS-091).
Must be a fresh keypair (no existing data).

accounts.name: config
docs: Config PDA

accounts.name: ct_config
docs: SSS-106: Confidential transfer config PDA.
Required (init) when FLAG_CONFIDENTIAL_TRANSFERS is set in params.feature_flags.
Must be omitted otherwise.

accounts.name: rent
docs: initialize_mint2 but kept for ABI stability with existing test clients.

instructions.name: migrate_config
docs: SSS-122: Migrate a StablecoinConfig PDA from v0 → current version.
Idempotent — safe to call on already-migrated configs.
Required before mint/burn/redeem on configs created by a pre-SSS-122 build.

accounts.name: authority
docs: The authority of the stablecoin — only they may trigger migration.

accounts.name: mint
docs: The SSS-token mint — used to verify config PDA seeds.

accounts.name: config
docs: The config PDA to migrate.
SAFETY: Declared as `UncheckedAccount` because Anchor's typed
deserialization (`Account<StablecoinConfig>`) would reject undersized
v0 accounts before realloc can run.  The handler manually verifies:
1. PDA seeds ([StablecoinConfig::SEED, mint]) match this account's key.
2. First 8 bytes match the StablecoinConfig Anchor discriminator.
3. The serialised `authority` field matches the `authority` signer.
4. Account is owned by this program (`owner` constraint below).

instructions.name: mint
docs: Mint tokens to a recipient. Caller must be a registered minter.

instructions.name: pause
docs: Pause the entire mint (SSS-2). No minting/burning while paused.

instructions.name: process_redemption
docs: Process a queued redemption entry. Keeper calls after min_delay_slots
have elapsed. Releases collateral to the redeemer and pays keeper reward.

accounts.name: keeper
docs: Keeper — receives lamport reward.

accounts.name: escrow_stable
docs: Per-entry escrow — stable tokens to burn.

accounts.name: reserve_vault
docs: Reserve vault — collateral source.

accounts.name: reserve_vault_authority
docs: Authority over the reserve vault (signs the collateral transfer out).

accounts.name: user_collateral_ata
docs: User's collateral ATA — receives reserve payout.

instructions.name: propose_action
docs: Open a governance proposal.
Authority opens a proposal for a specific `action` + optional `param`
and `target`.  The proposal collects YES votes from committee members
before it can be executed.

instructions.name: propose_timelocked_op
docs: Propose a timelocked admin operation (2-epoch delay by default).
`op_kind`: 1=TransferAuthority, 2=SetFeatureFlag, 3=ClearFeatureFlag.

instructions.name: redeem
docs: Redeem SSS tokens by burning them and releasing collateral (SSS-3 only).

accounts.name: sss_mint
docs: The SSS stablecoin mint

accounts.name: redeemer_sss_account
docs: Redeemer's SSS stablecoin token account (will be burned from)

accounts.name: collateral_mint
docs: The collateral token mint (e.g. USDC)

accounts.name: reserve_vault
docs: Reserve vault — releases collateral to redeemer (PDA-controlled)

accounts.name: redeemer_collateral
docs: Redeemer's collateral token account (receives collateral)

accounts.name: sss_token_program
docs: Token program for the SSS stablecoin (Token-2022)

accounts.name: collateral_token_program
docs: Token program for the collateral mint (may be Token or Token-2022)

instructions.name: register_collateral
docs: Register a new collateral type with per-collateral params (SSS-3, authority-only).
Creates the CollateralConfig PDA keyed by (sss_mint, collateral_mint).

accounts.name: config
docs: SSS stablecoin config — must be SSS-3 and signer must be authority.

accounts.name: sss_mint
docs: The SSS-3 stablecoin mint — identifies the config.

accounts.name: collateral_mint
docs: The collateral token mint to register.

accounts.name: collateral_config
docs: CollateralConfig PDA — created here.
Seeds: [b"collateral-config", sss_mint, collateral_mint]

instructions.name: register_legal_entity
docs: Authority-only: register the issuer's legal entity on-chain.
Creates an IssuerRegistry PDA and enables FLAG_LEGAL_REGISTRY.

instructions.name: remove_authorized_keeper
docs: BUG-015: Remove a keeper pubkey from the stability-fee keeper whitelist.

instructions.name: replenish_insurance_vault
docs: Permissionless: anyone may replenish the insurance vault after a draw.

instructions.name: revoke_minter
docs: Revoke a minter. Authority only.

instructions.name: seed_insurance_vault
docs: Deposit collateral into the insurance vault (anyone may seed; issuer must
hit min_seed_bps before minting is unlocked).

accounts.name: depositor_token_account
docs: Depositor's collateral token account (source).

accounts.name: vault_token_account
docs: The vault token account (destination).

instructions.name: set_backstop_params
docs: Authority-only: configure the insurance fund vault and max backstop draw cap.
Set `insurance_fund_pubkey` to `Pubkey::default()` to disable the backstop.
`max_backstop_bps`: max draw as pct of net supply in bps (0 = unlimited).

instructions.name: set_feature_flag
docs: Set a feature flag bit. Authority only. Pass the FLAG_* constant value.

instructions.name: set_mint_velocity_limit
docs: Set a per-epoch velocity limit for a registered minter.
`max_mint_per_epoch` = 0 disables the limit.  Authority-only.

instructions.name: set_oracle_params
docs: SSS-090: Configure oracle staleness and confidence parameters.
`max_age_secs`: max seconds a Pyth price may be old (0 = default 60s).
`max_conf_bps`: max confidence/price ratio in bps (0 = disabled).

instructions.name: set_psm_fee
docs: Set the PSM redemption fee in basis points (SSS-3 only). Authority-only.
0 = no fee.  Max 1000 bps (10%).

instructions.name: set_pyth_feed
docs: Register the expected Pyth price feed pubkey for this SSS-3 config.
After setting, CDP borrow and liquidate reject any other feed account.

instructions.name: set_spend_limit
docs: Set the per-tx spend limit and atomically enable FLAG_SPEND_POLICY.
`max_amount` must be > 0. Authority only.

instructions.name: set_stability_fee
docs: SSS-092: Set the annual stability fee (in basis points) for CDP borrows.
Max 2000 bps (20% p.a.).  0 = no fee (default).
Authority-only; takes effect on next `collect_stability_fee` call.

accounts.name: authority
docs: Must be the current authority for this config

instructions.name: submit_zk_proof
docs: Submit or refresh a ZK compliance proof for the calling user.
Creates or updates the caller's `VerificationRecord` PDA with an expiry
of `Clock::slot + ttl_slots` from `ZkComplianceConfig`.
`FLAG_ZK_COMPLIANCE` must already be enabled.  Any user may call this.
The transfer hook will enforce this record on each transfer.

accounts.name: verifier
docs: `ZkComplianceConfig.verifier_pubkey` is Some(_). Ignored otherwise.

instructions.name: thaw_account
docs: Thaw a frozen account. Caller must be compliance authority.

instructions.name: trigger_backstop
docs: Trigger the bad debt backstop after a liquidation leaves collateral < debt.
Draws up to `max_backstop_bps` of outstanding debt from the insurance fund.
Only callable by the config PDA (i.e. via CPI from `cdp_liquidate`).
Shortfall is computed entirely on-chain from CDP position + oracle price (BUG-031).
Emits `BadDebtTriggered`.

accounts.name: liquidation_authority
docs: Must be the config PDA — enforces that only the liquidation handler (which
holds a mutable borrow of config) can invoke trigger_backstop via CPI.

accounts.name: sss_mint
docs: SSS stablecoin mint — used for decimal scaling.

accounts.name: cdp_position
docs: CDP position of the borrower whose bad debt is being covered.
Seeds: [b"cdp-position", sss_mint, cdp_owner]

accounts.name: collateral_vault
docs: Collateral vault for this CDP — used to read remaining deposited_amount.
Seeds: [b"cdp-collateral-vault", sss_mint, cdp_owner, collateral_mint]

accounts.name: collateral_mint
docs: The collateral token mint — used for decimal scaling.

accounts.name: oracle_price_feed
docs: Oracle price feed account — validated by oracle::get_oracle_price via config.

accounts.name: insurance_fund
docs: Insurance fund token account — source of backstop collateral.

accounts.name: reserve_vault
docs: Reserve vault — destination for backstop collateral (same vault used by CDP).

accounts.name: insurance_fund_authority
docs: Insurance fund authority — must sign to allow transfer from insurance fund.

instructions.name: unpause
docs: Unpause the mint.

instructions.name: update_collateral_config
docs: Update an existing CollateralConfig PDA (SSS-3, authority-only).

accounts.name: config
docs: SSS stablecoin config — authority check.

accounts.name: sss_mint
docs: The SSS-3 stablecoin mint.

accounts.name: collateral_mint
docs: The collateral token mint.

accounts.name: collateral_config

instructions.name: update_interface_version

instructions.name: update_legal_entity

instructions.name: update_minter

instructions.name: update_redemption_queue
docs: Update RedemptionQueue parameters (authority-only).

instructions.name: vote_action

types.name: AuthorityRotationCancelled
docs: Emitted when the current authority cancels an in-flight rotation proposal.

docs: Emitted when the new_authority accepts the rotation after the timelock.

types.name: AuthorityRotationEmergencyRecovered
docs: Emitted when the backup_authority claims authority via emergency recovery.

types.name: AuthorityRotationProposed
docs: Emitted when an authority rotation is proposed.

types.name: BadDebtTriggered
docs: Emitted when a bad-debt backstop draw occurs.

fields.name: sss_mint
docs: The SSS stablecoin mint this backstop covers.

fields.name: backstop_amount
docs: Amount of collateral transferred from the insurance fund (native token units).

fields.name: computed_shortfall
docs: Computed on-chain shortfall (debt − collateral value, in SSS native units).

fields.name: remaining_shortfall
docs: Remaining shortfall after backstop draw (0 if fully covered).

fields.name: net_supply
docs: Net supply (outstanding debt) at trigger time.

types.name: CdpBorrowed
docs: Emitted when SSS tokens are borrowed against collateral.

types.name: CdpCollateralDeposited
docs: Emitted when collateral is deposited into a CDP vault.

types.name: CdpLiquidateParams
docs: Params for the `cdp_liquidate` instruction.
- `min_collateral_amount`: slippage protection — reverts if less collateral
would be received.  Pass 0 for no protection (backward-compatible).
- `partial_repay_amount`: when > 0, only this many debt tokens are burned and
just enough collateral to cover them (plus bonus) is seized.  When 0 the
entire debt is burned (full liquidation).

types.name: CdpLiquidated
docs: Emitted when a CDP position is liquidated (SSS-110 circuit breaker compatible).

fields.name: ratio_bps
docs: Collateral ratio at time of liquidation (in basis points).

types.name: CdpPosition
docs: CDP position PDA — one per user, single-collateral per position (SSS-054 fix).
Seeds: ["cdp-position", sss_mint, user]

fields.name: config
docs: The SSS stablecoin config this CDP borrows against

fields.name: sss_mint
docs: The SSS stablecoin mint

fields.name: owner
docs: The user who owns this CDP

fields.name: debt_amount
docs: Total SSS-3 tokens currently borrowed (outstanding debt)

fields.name: collateral_mint
docs: The single collateral mint for this position (set on first borrow, immutable).
Enforces 1:1 CollateralVault-to-CdpPosition to prevent liquidation insolvency.

fields.name: last_fee_accrual
docs: SSS-092: Unix timestamp of the last stability fee accrual for this position.
Initialised to 0; set on first borrow and updated each time fees are collected.

fields.name: accrued_fees
docs: SSS-092: Total stability fees accrued (in SSS token native units) but not yet
collected by the protocol.  Debtor must repay principal + accrued_fees.

types.name: CdpRepaid
docs: Emitted when SSS tokens are repaid and collateral is released.

types.name: ChannelDisputed
docs: Emitted when a channel is placed in dispute.

types.name: ChannelForceClosed
docs: Emitted when a channel is force-closed by the initiator after timeout.

types.name: ChannelOpened
docs: Emitted when a new agent payment channel is opened.

types.name: ChannelSettled
docs: Emitted when a channel is mutually settled.

types.name: CollateralConfig
docs: Per-collateral configuration PDA.
Seeds: [b"collateral-config", sss_mint, collateral_mint]
Stores per-collateral LTV, liquidation threshold/bonus, deposit cap, and
a whitelist flag.  `cdp_deposit_collateral` reads this when it is passed
as an optional account.

fields.name: sss_mint
docs: The SSS-3 stablecoin mint this config belongs to.

fields.name: collateral_mint
docs: The collateral token mint.

fields.name: whitelisted
docs: When false, CDP deposits for this mint are rejected.

fields.name: max_ltv_bps
docs: Maximum loan-to-value ratio in basis points (e.g. 7500 = 75%).

fields.name: liquidation_threshold_bps
docs: Collateral ratio below which a position becomes liquidatable.
Must be > max_ltv_bps.

fields.name: liquidation_bonus_bps
docs: Extra collateral awarded to the liquidator, in basis points.

fields.name: max_deposit_cap
docs: Maximum total deposited amount for this collateral (0 = unlimited).

fields.name: total_deposited
docs: Running total of collateral deposited through CDP (informational).

types.name: CollateralLiquidated
docs: SSS-100: Emitted on every CDP liquidation (full and partial) with multi-collateral details.

fields.name: mint
docs: The SSS stablecoin mint.

fields.name: collateral_mint
docs: The specific collateral mint seized.

fields.name: cdp_owner
docs: The CDP owner whose position was (partially) liquidated.

fields.name: liquidator
docs: The liquidator who initiated the liquidation.

fields.name: debt_burned
docs: Amount of SSS debt burned.

fields.name: collateral_seized
docs: Amount of collateral transferred to the liquidator.

fields.name: ratio_before_bps
docs: Collateral ratio before liquidation (basis points).

fields.name: partial
docs: Whether this was a partial liquidation (true) or full (false).

fields.name: bonus_bps
docs: Liquidation bonus applied in basis points (from CollateralConfig or global default).

types.name: CollateralVault
docs: Collateral vault PDA — one per (user, collateral_mint).
Seeds: ["cdp-collateral-vault", user, collateral_mint]

fields.name: owner
docs: The user who owns this vault

fields.name: collateral_mint
docs: The SPL token mint for this collateral type

fields.name: vault_token_account
docs: The token account that holds collateral (owned by the vault PDA)

fields.name: deposited_amount
docs: Total collateral deposited (in collateral token's native units)

types.name: ConfidentialTransferConfig
docs: Confidential transfer config PDA — one per mint when FLAG_CONFIDENTIAL_TRANSFERS is set.
Seeds: [b"ct-config", mint]
Stores the issuer's auditor ElGamal pubkey for confidential transfers.
The auditor can decrypt all transfer amounts; external observers cannot.
This is the foundation for Token-2022 ConfidentialTransferMint extension integration.
The actual ConfidentialTransferMint extension wiring is a follow-up (SSS-107).

fields.name: mint
docs: The SSS mint this config belongs to.

fields.name: auditor_elgamal_pubkey
docs: The issuer's ElGamal public key used to audit (decrypt) all transfer amounts.
Must be a valid ElGamal pubkey on the Ristretto255 curve (32 bytes).

fields.name: auto_approve_new_accounts
docs: When true, new token accounts are automatically approved for confidential transfers.
When false, the authority must manually approve each account.

types.name: DaoCommitteeConfig
docs: DAO Committee Config PDA — tracks committee members and proposal counter.
Seeds: [b"dao-committee", config]
Initialized by `init_dao_committee`; managed by authority.

fields.name: config
docs: The stablecoin config this committee governs.

fields.name: members
docs: Ordered list of committee member pubkeys (max 10).

fields.name: quorum
docs: Minimum YES votes required to pass a proposal (must be ≤ members.len()).

fields.name: next_proposal_id
docs: Next proposal ID to assign (auto-incremented on `propose_action`).

types.name: GuardianConfig
docs: GuardianConfig PDA — one per stablecoin config.
Seeds: [b"guardian-config", config_pubkey]
Stores up to 7 guardian pubkeys and a threshold.  Guardians may only
pause or unpause the mint — they cannot mint, burn, or alter fees.

fields.name: config
docs: The StablecoinConfig this guardian set governs.

fields.name: guardians
docs: Registered guardian pubkeys (1–7).

fields.name: threshold
docs: Minimum votes required to execute a pause proposal.

fields.name: next_proposal_id
docs: Auto-incrementing ID assigned to the next PauseProposal.

fields.name: pending_lift_votes
docs: Votes accumulated for lifting the current pause via full-quorum path.
Reset to empty when the pause is lifted.

fields.name: guardian_pause_active
docs: BUG-018: Set to true when a guardian-quorum pause is active.
Authority alone CANNOT lift the pause while this is true + timelock active.

fields.name: guardian_pause_unlocks_at
docs: BUG-018: Unix timestamp after which authority may lift a guardian-initiated
pause unilaterally (GUARDIAN_PAUSE_AUTHORITY_OVERRIDE_DELAY seconds after pause).
Zero when no guardian pause is active.

types.name: GuardianPauseAuthorityOverride
docs: Emitted when authority lifts a guardian pause after the timelock has expired.

fields.name: timestamp
docs: Unix timestamp when override occurred.

types.name: GuardianPauseLifted
docs: Emitted when a guardian-imposed pause is lifted.

types.name: GuardianPauseProposed
docs: Emitted when a guardian opens a new pause proposal.

types.name: GuardianPauseVoted
docs: Emitted when a guardian votes on an open pause proposal.

types.name: InitializeParams
docs: Parameters for initializing a new stablecoin.

fields.name: preset
docs: 1 = SSS-1, 2 = SSS-2, 3 = SSS-3 (reserve-backed)

fields.name: decimals
docs: Token decimals

fields.name: name
docs: Human-readable token name

fields.name: symbol
docs: Token symbol (e.g. USDC)

fields.name: uri
docs: Metadata URI

fields.name: transfer_hook_program
docs: Transfer hook program (required for SSS-2)

fields.name: collateral_mint
docs: SSS-3: collateral token mint (e.g. USDC mint address)

fields.name: reserve_vault
docs: SSS-3: reserve vault token account address

fields.name: max_supply
docs: Maximum token supply (None or 0 = unlimited)

fields.name: feature_flags
docs: Initial feature flags bitmask (see FLAG_* constants). Optional — defaults to 0.

fields.name: auditor_elgamal_pubkey
docs: Auditor ElGamal pubkey for confidential transfers (required if FLAG_CONFIDENTIAL_TRANSFERS is set)

fields.name: admin_timelock_delay
docs: Override the admin timelock delay (slots). None = use DEFAULT_ADMIN_TIMELOCK_DELAY.
Pass Some(0) in test environments to allow direct admin calls without going
through the propose/execute timelock flow.

fields.name: squads_multisig
docs: SSS-147A: Squads V4 multisig PDA for FLAG_SQUADS_AUTHORITY.
Required when preset == 3 — SSS-3 stablecoins must be governed by a multisig.
Optional for SSS-1 and SSS-2 (but may still be provided to enable FLAG_SQUADS_AUTHORITY).

types.name: InsuranceDrawn
docs: Emitted when the insurance vault is drawn to cover protocol losses.

types.name: InsuranceVault
docs: First-loss insurance vault PDA — holds collateral seeded by the issuer.
Seeds: [b"insurance-vault", sss_mint]

fields.name: sss_mint
docs: The SSS stablecoin mint this vault protects.

fields.name: vault_token_account
docs: The token account holding the vault's collateral.

fields.name: min_seed_bps
docs: Minimum seed as basis points of net_supply (e.g. 200 = 2%).

fields.name: current_balance
docs: Current token balance in the vault (in collateral native units).

fields.name: total_drawn
docs: Total tokens drawn from the vault to cover bad debt.

fields.name: max_draw_per_event_bps
docs: Per-event draw cap in bps of net_supply (0 = no cap).

fields.name: adequately_seeded
docs: True when current_balance >= required seed amount.

types.name: InsuranceVaultReplenished
docs: Emitted when the insurance vault is replenished.

types.name: InsuranceVaultSeeded
docs: Emitted when the insurance vault is seeded.

types.name: InterfaceVersion
docs: Interface version PDA — external callers check this before invoking SSS via CPI.
Seeds: ["interface-version", sss_mint]
Callers should read this PDA and verify:
- `version` matches their expected version (breaking changes bump version)
- `active` is true (protocol not deprecated)
Breaking interface changes require a new program address per Solana convention,
but the version field gives callers a cheap on-chain safety check.

fields.name: mint
docs: The SSS mint this interface applies to

fields.name: version
docs: Current interface version (1 = initial CPI composability standard)

fields.name: active
docs: Whether this interface is active; false = deprecated / use new program

fields.name: namespace
docs: Interface namespace used for discriminator derivation
sha256("sss_mint_interface:mint")[..8] => mint discriminator
sha256("sss_mint_interface:burn")[..8] => burn discriminator

types.name: IssuerRegistry
docs: On-chain issuer registry for regulatory traceability.
Seeds: [b"issuer_registry", config_pubkey]
Optional PDA enabled by FLAG_LEGAL_REGISTRY (bit 24). Allows regulators
and on-chain programs to verify the issuer's legal entity details without
trusting off-chain data. The `attestor` is a notary or lawyer whose Pubkey
co-signs the record via `attest_legal_entity`.

fields.name: config
docs: StablecoinConfig this registry belongs to.

fields.name: legal_entity_hash
docs: SHA-256 hash of the legal entity document (e.g. articles of incorporation).

fields.name: jurisdiction
docs: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code encoded as UTF-8 bytes, zero-padded to 4.

fields.name: registration_number_hash
docs: SHA-256 hash of the jurisdiction registration number (for privacy).

fields.name: attestor
docs: Pubkey of the notary/lawyer who will attest this record.

fields.name: attested_slot
docs: Slot at which the attestor signed (0 = not yet attested).

fields.name: expiry_slot
docs: Slot after which this record is considered expired (0 = no expiry).

fields.name: attested
docs: True after `attest_legal_entity` succeeds.

types.name: LegalEntityAttested
docs: Emitted when the attestor co-signs the registry record.

types.name: LegalEntityRegistered
docs: Emitted when a legal entity record is first registered.

types.name: LegalEntityUpdated
docs: Emitted when the authority updates the registry (resets attestation).

types.name: MintHaltedByPoRBreach
docs: Emitted when minting is halted because the reserve ratio breaches the minimum.

types.name: MintVelocityUpdated
docs: SSS-093: Emitted when a minter's per-epoch velocity limit is updated.

types.name: MinterInfo
docs: Per-minter configuration — minters are PDAs keyed by [config, minter_pubkey].

fields.name: config
docs: The stablecoin config this minter belongs to

fields.name: minter
docs: Minter wallet

fields.name: cap
docs: Maximum tokens this minter is allowed to mint (0 = unlimited)

fields.name: minted
docs: Total minted by this minter so far

fields.name: max_mint_per_epoch
docs: SSS-093: Maximum tokens this minter may mint per epoch (0 = unlimited).
Prevents flash-mint attacks by rate-limiting per Solana epoch.

fields.name: minted_this_epoch
docs: SSS-093: Amount minted in the current epoch (resets when epoch advances).

fields.name: last_epoch_reset
docs: SSS-093: The epoch slot-number when `minted_this_epoch` was last reset.
Solana epoch = `clock.epoch` (u64).

types.name: OracleConsensus
docs: Multi-oracle consensus PDA — aggregates N price sources into a single
median/TWAP consensus price.
Seeds: [b"oracle-consensus", sss_mint]

fields.name: mint
docs: The SSS stablecoin mint this consensus belongs to.

fields.name: min_oracles
docs: Minimum number of valid sources required for consensus.

fields.name: outlier_threshold_bps
docs: Maximum deviation in bps before a source is considered an outlier.

fields.name: source_count
docs: Number of currently active sources (non-default feed).

fields.name: max_age_slots
docs: Maximum age in slots for a source price to be included in consensus.

fields.name: sources
docs: Fixed-size array of oracle sources (MAX_SOURCES slots).

fields.name: last_consensus_price
docs: The last computed consensus price (median of valid sources).

fields.name: last_consensus_slot
docs: Slot at which the consensus was last updated.

fields.name: twap_price
docs: Running TWAP price (exponential moving average).

fields.name: twap_last_slot
docs: Slot of last TWAP update.

types.name: OracleSource
docs: A single oracle source entry stored inline in OracleConsensus.
oracle_type: 0=Pyth, 1=Switchboard, 2=Custom.

fields.name: oracle_type
docs: Oracle type: 0=Pyth, 1=Switchboard, 2=Custom.

fields.name: feed
docs: The oracle feed account address (Pubkey::default = empty slot).

types.name: PauseProposal
docs: PauseProposal PDA — one per proposal.
Seeds: [b"pause-proposal", config_pubkey, proposal_id.to_le_bytes()]
Tracks YES votes on a pending emergency-pause proposal.
Once `votes.len() >= threshold` the proposal is auto-executed and
`executed` is set to `true`.

fields.name: config
docs: The StablecoinConfig this proposal targets.

fields.name: proposal_id
docs: Sequential ID matching the `GuardianConfig.next_proposal_id` at creation.

fields.name: proposer
docs: Guardian who opened the proposal (already counted as 1 vote).

fields.name: reason
docs: Freeform reason bytes (e.g. incident hash or ASCII string).

fields.name: votes
docs: Guardians who have voted YES (no duplicates).

fields.name: threshold
docs: Voting threshold copied from GuardianConfig at proposal creation.

fields.name: executed
docs: True when the threshold was reached and the pause was applied.

types.name: ProbabilisticCommitmentCreated
docs: Emitted when a new probabilistic commitment is created (funds locked in escrow).

fields.name: config
docs: StablecoinConfig PDA this commitment belongs to.

fields.name: commitment_id
docs: Unique commitment id (caller-provided, scoped to config).

fields.name: issuer
docs: Account that locked the funds.

fields.name: claimant
docs: Account authorised to claim on proof.

fields.name: stable_mint
docs: SSS stablecoin mint.

fields.name: committed_amount
docs: Total tokens committed.

fields.name: condition_hash
docs: Condition hash the proof must match.

fields.name: expiry_slot
docs: Slot after which issuer may claim a refund.

types.name: ProbabilisticCommitmentResolved
docs: Emitted on every resolution (full or partial) of a probabilistic commitment.

fields.name: config
docs: StablecoinConfig PDA.

fields.name: commitment_id
docs: Commitment id being resolved.

fields.name: claimant
docs: Claimant who received the tokens.

fields.name: amount_released
docs: Amount released to claimant in this resolution.

fields.name: partial
docs: True if this was a partial resolution (remainder returned to issuer).

types.name: ProposalAction
docs: Action kinds that a DAO committee proposal may authorize.
Encoded as a u8 discriminant to keep the PDA small.

types.name: ProposalPda
docs: DAO Governance Proposal PDA.
Seeds: [b"dao-proposal", config, proposal_id.to_le_bytes()]
Created by `propose_action`, voted on by `vote_action`, executed by `execute_action`.

fields.name: config
docs: The stablecoin config this proposal governs.

fields.name: proposal_id
docs: Monotonically increasing proposal index for this config (0-based).

fields.name: proposer
docs: Who created the proposal (must be the current authority).

fields.name: action
docs: The action to execute when the proposal passes.

fields.name: param
docs: Generic u64 parameter (flag bits for Set/ClearFeatureFlag; cap for UpdateMinter; 0 otherwise).

fields.name: target
docs: Target pubkey (minter key for UpdateMinter/RevokeMinter; default otherwise).

fields.name: votes
docs: Set of committee member keys that have voted YES (max 10 members).
We store votes inline to avoid extra accounts; for simplicity, duplicate
vote attempts are rejected at the instruction level.

fields.name: quorum
docs: Quorum: how many YES votes are required to execute.

fields.name: executed
docs: Whether this proposal has been executed (one-shot).

fields.name: cancelled
docs: Whether this proposal has been cancelled.

types.name: PsmFeeUpdated
docs: SSS-093: Emitted when the PSM redemption fee is updated.

types.name: PsmSwapEvent
docs: SSS-093: Emitted on every PSM redeem (deposit_collateral→mint is fee-free).

fields.name: sss_burned
docs: SSS tokens burned by the redeemer.

fields.name: collateral_out
docs: Collateral tokens released to the redeemer (sss_burned - fee_collected).

fields.name: fee_collected
docs: Collateral retained in vault as fee.

fields.name: fee_bps
docs: Fee rate at time of swap in basis points.

types.name: RedemptionEntry
docs: Individual entry in the redemption queue.
Seeds: [b"redemption-entry", sss_mint, queue_index.to_le_bytes()]

fields.name: queue_index
docs: Index within the queue (matches queue_tail at enqueue time).

fields.name: owner
docs: The wallet that submitted this redemption.

fields.name: amount
docs: Amount of SSS tokens to redeem.

fields.name: enqueue_slot
docs: Slot at which the request was enqueued.

fields.name: slot_hash_seed
docs: Hash seed used for front-run protection (first 8 bytes of slot hash).

fields.name: fulfilled
docs: True when the redemption has been fulfilled.

fields.name: cancelled
docs: True when the redemption was cancelled by the owner.

types.name: RedemptionQueue
docs: Redemption queue PDA — front-run-protected FIFO queue.
Seeds: [b"redemption-queue", sss_mint]

fields.name: sss_mint
docs: The SSS stablecoin mint this queue serves.

fields.name: queue_head
docs: Index of the next entry to be fulfilled (FIFO head).

fields.name: queue_tail
docs: Index of the next entry to be enqueued (tail).

fields.name: min_delay_slots
docs: Minimum slots a request must wait before it can be fulfilled.

fields.name: max_queue_depth
docs: Maximum number of pending entries.

fields.name: max_redemption_per_slot_bps
docs: Maximum redemption per slot expressed as bps of net_supply (0 = no cap).

fields.name: last_slot_processed
docs: Slot at which the last fulfillment occurred (for slot-cap tracking).

fields.name: slot_redemption_total
docs: Total amount redeemed in `last_slot_processed`.

fields.name: keeper_reward_lamports
docs: SOL reward paid to the keeper who fulfills a redemption entry.

fields.name: max_deposit_cap
docs: 0 = unlimited

types.name: StablecoinConfig
docs: Global stablecoin configuration (one per mint).

fields.name: mint
docs: Mint address (Token-2022)

fields.name: authority
docs: Overall authority (can update roles, update minters)

fields.name: compliance_authority
docs: Compliance authority (can freeze/thaw, manages blacklist on-chain)

fields.name: preset
docs: Preset: 1 = SSS-1 (minimal), 2 = SSS-2 (compliant)

fields.name: paused
docs: Whether the mint is currently paused (SSS-2 feature)

fields.name: total_minted
docs: Total tokens minted (sum, not accounting for burns)

fields.name: total_burned
docs: Total tokens burned

fields.name: transfer_hook_program
docs: Transfer hook program (SSS-2 only; Pubkey::default if SSS-1/3)

fields.name: collateral_mint
docs: SSS-3: collateral token mint (e.g. USDC; Pubkey::default if SSS-1/2)

fields.name: reserve_vault
docs: SSS-3: reserve vault token account address (Pubkey::default if SSS-1/2)

fields.name: total_collateral
docs: SSS-3: total collateral deposited into the reserve vault

fields.name: max_supply
docs: Maximum token supply (0 = unlimited)

fields.name: pending_authority
docs: Pending authority for two-step authority transfer (Pubkey::default if none)

fields.name: pending_compliance_authority
docs: Pending compliance authority for two-step transfer (Pubkey::default if none)

fields.name: feature_flags
docs: Bitmask of enabled feature flags. See FLAG_* constants.

fields.name: max_transfer_amount
docs: Maximum tokens per transfer when FLAG_SPEND_POLICY is set (0 = policy
not yet configured; admin must call `set_spend_limit` before enabling).

fields.name: expected_pyth_feed
docs: SSS-085: Expected Pyth price feed Pubkey for CDP operations.
When non-default, `cdp_borrow_stable` and `cdp_liquidate` reject any
pyth_price_feed account that does not match this key exactly.
Set via `set_pyth_feed` (authority-only).  Default = Pubkey::default()
(validation disabled; set before mainnet).

fields.name: admin_op_mature_slot
docs: SSS-085: Slot at which the pending timelocked admin operation matures.
0 = no pending operation.  Set by `propose_timelocked_op`; cleared by
`execute_timelocked_op` or `cancel_timelocked_op`.

fields.name: admin_op_kind
docs: SSS-085: Discriminant for the pending timelocked admin operation.
0 = none; matches AdminOpKind enum.

fields.name: admin_op_param
docs: SSS-085: Generic u64 parameter for the pending timelocked admin op.

fields.name: admin_op_target
docs: SSS-085: Target pubkey for the pending timelocked admin op.

fields.name: admin_timelock_delay
docs: SSS-085: Minimum slot delay for admin timelock (default 2 epochs ≈ 432 000 slots).
Can be set at init or by authority via `set_timelock_delay`.

fields.name: max_oracle_age_secs
docs: SSS-090: Maximum Pyth price age in seconds for CDP operations.
0 = use hardcoded default (60 s).  Set via `set_oracle_params`.

fields.name: max_oracle_conf_bps
docs: SSS-090: Maximum acceptable Pyth confidence interval as a fraction of price,
expressed in basis points (e.g. 100 = 1%).  0 = disabled (no conf check).
Set via `set_oracle_params`.

fields.name: stability_fee_bps
docs: SSS-092: Annual stability fee in basis points (e.g. 50 = 0.5% p.a.).
Accrues on outstanding CDP debt; collected via `collect_stability_fee`.
0 = no stability fee (default).

fields.name: redemption_fee_bps
docs: SSS-093: PSM redemption fee in basis points (e.g. 10 = 0.1%).
Deducted from collateral released on `redeem`.  Fee stays in vault.
0 = no fee (default).  Set via `set_psm_fee` (authority-only).

fields.name: insurance_fund_pubkey
docs: SSS-097: Insurance fund vault — token account that holds backstop reserves.
Pubkey::default() = backstop disabled.  Set via `set_backstop_params` (authority-only).

fields.name: max_backstop_bps
docs: SSS-097: Maximum backstop draw as a fraction of total outstanding debt,
expressed in basis points (e.g. 500 = 5% of net supply).
0 = unlimited (draw full shortfall up to insurance fund balance).

fields.name: auditor_elgamal_pubkey
docs: SSS-106: Auditor ElGamal pubkey for confidential transfers.
All-zero if FLAG_CONFIDENTIAL_TRANSFERS is not enabled.

fields.name: oracle_type
docs: SSS-119: Oracle type discriminant. 0=Pyth (default), 1=Switchboard, 2=Custom.
Set via `set_oracle_config` (authority-only).

fields.name: oracle_feed
docs: SSS-119: Generic oracle feed account address used by the oracle abstraction layer.
For Pyth: the Pyth price feed account (overrides expected_pyth_feed when set).
For Custom: the CustomPriceFeed PDA address.
Pubkey::default() = validation deferred to expected_pyth_feed (backward compat).

fields.name: supply_cap_locked
docs: SSS-147: When true (set at initialize for preset==3), the max_supply / supply_cap
is immutable after initialization.  Prevents the authority from later expanding
the supply cap to defeat the SSS-3 trust-minimized minting invariant.

fields.name: version
docs: Config version — incremented when breaking changes are made to the config layout.
Instructions that require a minimum version check against this field.

fields.name: min_reserve_ratio_bps
docs: SSS-BUG-008: Minimum reserve ratio in basis points (0 = no minimum).
CDP minting is blocked when the reserve ratio drops below this threshold.

fields.name: travel_rule_threshold
docs: SSS-127: Travel Rule transfer threshold in native token units.
0 = Travel Rule disabled regardless of FLAG_TRAVEL_RULE.

fields.name: sanctions_oracle
docs: SSS-128: Sanctions oracle pubkey (compliance provider).
Pubkey::default() = sanctions oracle not configured.

fields.name: sanctions_max_staleness_slots
docs: SSS-128: Maximum age in slots for a SanctionsRecord to be considered fresh.
0 = use hardcoded default.

fields.name: authorized_keepers
docs: BUG-015: Whitelisted stability-fee / circuit-breaker keeper pubkeys (max 8).

fields.name: squads_multisig
docs: SSS-134: Squads V4 multisig PDA address when FLAG_SQUADS_AUTHORITY is set.
Pubkey::default() = squads authority not configured.

fields.name: expected_upgrade_authority
docs: SSS-150: Expected BPF upgrade authority pubkey for upgrade guard enforcement.
Pubkey::default() = guard not set.

fields.name: max_deposit_cap
docs: 0 = unlimited

types.name: UpdateRolesParams
docs: Parameters for updating authorities.

types.name: VerificationRecord
docs: Per-user ZK verification record PDA — one per (mint, user).
Seeds: [b"zk-verification", sss_mint, user]
Created or refreshed by `submit_zk_proof`.  The transfer hook checks this
PDA whenever FLAG_ZK_COMPLIANCE is active: if the record is absent or
`expires_at_slot <= Clock::slot`, the transfer is rejected.
Authority may close expired records via `close_verification_record` to
reclaim rent.

fields.name: sss_mint
docs: The SSS stablecoin mint this record is scoped to.

fields.name: user
docs: The wallet that submitted the proof.

fields.name: expires_at_slot
docs: The slot at which this record expires (exclusive).
Valid while `Clock::get().slot < expires_at_slot`.

types.name: WorkProofSubmitted
docs: Emitted when a work proof is submitted to a channel.

types.name: YieldCollateralConfig
docs: Yield-collateral config PDA — one per stablecoin mint.
Seeds: [b"yield-collateral", sss_mint]
Stores a whitelist of SPL token mints that may be used as yield-bearing
collateral in CDP deposits when FLAG_YIELD_COLLATERAL is enabled.
Maximum 8 whitelisted mints — covers all practical yield-token variants
(stSOL, mSOL, jitoSOL, bSOL, etc.) without unbounded Vec heap.

fields.name: sss_mint
docs: The SSS stablecoin mint this config belongs to

fields.name: whitelisted_mints
docs: Whitelisted yield-bearing SPL token mints (max 8)

types.name: ZkComplianceConfig
docs: ZK-compliance config PDA — one per stablecoin mint.
Seeds: [b"zk-compliance-config", sss_mint]
Stores protocol-wide settings for ZK proof verification.
Created by `init_zk_compliance`; enables FLAG_ZK_COMPLIANCE.
SSS-2 only.

fields.name: sss_mint
docs: The SSS stablecoin mint this config belongs to.

fields.name: ttl_slots
docs: Number of slots a VerificationRecord is valid after submission.
Default: 1500 slots (~10 minutes at 400ms/slot).

fields.name: verifier_pubkey
docs: Optional compliance oracle / verifier pubkey.
When `Some(vk)`, `submit_zk_proof` requires a co-signature from `vk`
to prevent self-issued proofs. When `None`, any caller may submit.
Set during `init_zk_compliance` and cannot be changed after init.
